title: CS Monitor: Dozens of FTCA Excessive-Force Claims Filed Against ICE Nationwide
excerpt: An investigation by the Christian Science Monitor found more than a dozen Federal Tort Claims Act filings from June–December 2025 alleging ICE and Border Patrol agents assaulted or detained people without cause, leaving claimants with bruises, lacerations, and head injuries.
content:
A February 3, 2026, investigation by the Christian Science Monitor reviewed more than a dozen Federal Tort Claims Act filings, dated June through December 2025, in which people alleged that ICE and U.S. Border Patrol personnel assaulted or detained them without cause.

Claimants reported bruises, lacerations, and head injuries. Almost a dozen alleged they were tackled or thrown to the ground. Multiple claimants said federal personnel pushed their knees into detainees' faces, necks, or backs; one man alleged agents pepper-sprayed him before repeatedly punching him in the face and head.

  • Civil rights lawyers reported 'a huge wave of people reaching out with excessive force claims' in the wake of the surge in immigration operations.
  • Attorneys said the increase in claims suggests excessive force is a national problem extending far beyond Minneapolis.
  • No central database tracks FTCA claims; ICE had not released its 2025 annual report containing claim statistics.
  • The surge in at-large arrests in interior cities has made civilian encounters with federal law enforcement far more common than in prior years, experts told the Monitor.
Patrick Jaicomo, Institute for Justice: 'All of this really highlights the lack of federal accountability.'
mediaPlaceholder: Stacked FTCA complaint filings with redacted claimant names showing injury descriptions
